There are players who consistently rank in the TOP 50 every season in the Survivor tier, named Tajik_AgresoR and Akai_AJ. These players regularly play with Tier 1/2 accounts, which get banned immediately after being reported. Today, these players played with Juanito_ and 2Speed4U. Immediately after my report, both players were banned. However, these "Survivors" managed to play 8 matches with them. It’s clear they have many accounts through which they get boosted using cheats, but this is too much. A huge number of players are doing this, and PUBG ranked matches are unplayable because of it. Honorable mentions for players who also do this and aren’t punished for it: LEBANON_forameh and his LEBANON CLAN, CN1 CLAN, MEC CLAN, XAOC CLAN. I’ve been having a fantastic time with PUBG. The fact that it’s now free-to-play is honestly outstanding—it makes such a high-quality battle royale accessible to everyone. The graphics still hold up really well, and the atmosphere across the maps feels immersive and intense.

Gameplay-wise, it’s exactly what you want: tactical, rewarding, and punishing in the right ways. Gunplay feels solid, and every match has that classic PUBG tension where one mistake can cost you everything.

As a newer player (and I’d say a fairly capable one), my only real issue has been with teammate matchmaking. It can be a bit hit and miss—sometimes you get a great squad, other times it feels completely unbalanced. That said, when the team clicks, the experience is top-tier.

The most complex, in-depth, open realistic sim RPG worlds I ever played... and I have done many of them. THAT being said, IF you use mods and take time to learn the game mechanics, you may enjoy this game so much more. I am 249 hours in, and like most open world RPGs, I have stayed away from most main quests and just immersed in the world... downside, with mods, expect crashes now and then.. save often, manually. Recommend jetpack mod, and every immersion and life-sim mods you can get, and load them manually, it's easy. Avoid Vortex, it will make a mess. Have fun!

I grew up playing Counter-Strike 1.3, 1.5, 1.6, and Source. I also played Global Offensive.
I loved playing CS back when you could install your own weapon skins for free and choose your own tags for free.
It has been years since then and I am trying to get used to the game again. I have been playing COD and BF. So it is very hard to relearn the game.
That being said, I LOVE THIS GAME STILL.
I just wish I had enough money to be as addicted to the cases as I am. I have only opened maybe 7.
Also I don't think you should have to purchase the cases AND the keys. The cases should be free once in a while when you level up OR should have to buy the cases, but once you purchase them you should be able to open them!

Elden Ring is not a masterpiece like everyone says. It's a journey of challenging highs and frustrating lows. 

It's your typical From-Software Dark Souls game. Just with a different coat of paint, and an open world to explore.

If you buy this game, you should know what you're getting into. It is a hard game. I'm mixed on this entry. 
I feel like some of the bosses and enemies are just overtuned.

A lot of these bosses have crazy combo chains that vary too much for me to keep track of. Lots of delayed moves and odd approach angles. Once I lose enough. I just give up and go explore instead.  Usually I come back when feel I've gotten stronger and have better gear/upgrades.

Kinda how it works. It's not a fun feeling dying a ton and losing all your runes until you improve. Unfortunately. This is a souls standard. If you have to. Summon help. Again. Casuals beware. 
This game will cook you until you cook it.


That's gonna put a lot of people off. I'm a casual. I don't enjoy souls style games, but I wanted to see if I stood a chance in Elden Ring.  On sale, of course. Under $40. Purchased elsewhere.

 I'm not paying $70 to be mad all day. As  for performance? It runs like garbage without serious setting tweaks on the Steam Deck. So get it on a main rig if you can.

Can't reccomend for casuals. Can reccomend for people who like or are familar with souls style games.
